rationale: The cited 2005 ketchup paper develops a joint household-demand/manufacturer-retailer supply framework, and the 2007 citing paper’s design similarly estimates household demand and recovers margins under alternative manufacturer-retailer pricing games. The citing note also explicitly says it analyzes cereals and “replicate[s] substantive conclusions in the ketchup category,” making this citation plausibly methodological/foundational rather than a generic background mention.
